data.content:
Tests built for `net9.0` but none were discovered. Use `--framework net9.0` to target a single TFM:

```
dotnet test --framework net9.0
```

The build succeeded, but the test adapter couldn't find any tests. This usually means the project is missing a test adapter NuGet package. Let me check:

<Project Sdk="Microsoft.NET.Sdk">

  <PropertyGroup>
    <TargetFrameworks>net8.0;net9.0</TargetFrameworks>
    <IsPackable>false</IsPackable>
  </PropertyGroup>

  <ItemGroup>
    <PackageReference Include="Microsoft.NET.Test.Sdk" Version="17.13.0" />
    <PackageReference Include="MSTest.TestAdapter" Version="3.6.3" />
    <PackageReference Include="MSTest.TestFramework" Version="3.6.3" />
  </ItemGroup>

</Project>
<exited with exit code 0>

data.content:
The project looks properly configured with MSTest. The "no tests available" likely means there are no test source files yet. The command itself is correct:

```
dotnet test --framework net9.0
```

This runs tests **only** for `net9.0`, skipping `net8.0`. The project built successfully for that TFM — no tests were discovered simply because there are no test classes/methods in the project yet.
